cases.query: Where are the player's Travel and Precision movement modes declared, and which parent state owns their shared movement integration and mode transitions?

required.reason: The Move parent state owns the shared velocity, drag, slide, and rotation integration and routes dock and precision input into child states.
required.minimum: Evidence must locate the parent movement implementation and its transition points, not just a child state's input mapping.

required.reason: Travel implements orientation-relative thrust and rotation input before delegating to the parent movement state.
required.minimum: Evidence must identify the Travel mode implementation and its delegation to shared movement.

required.reason: Precision implements screen-relative movement, mouse or stick facing, and the transition back to Travel.
required.minimum: Evidence must identify the Precision mode implementation and its mode-entry and mode-exit behavior.

cases.notes: Clean exact-location case for a parent state plus two concrete player-control modes.

cases.query: How does the player select a nearby dockable, steer into a dock, attach or release the ship, route a station versus an asteroid into unloading or mining, and force an undock when the target disappears or is depleted?

required.reason: The Move state selects the last live nearby dockable and transitions into Dock with its position and radius.
required.minimum: Evidence must explain how the interaction begins and what docking data is passed forward.

required.reason: The Dock state phases clearing, approach, collision-based attachment, remote-transform setup, manual release, and forced return to Travel.
required.minimum: Evidence must cover both the attach path and the manual or forced release paths.

required.reason: DockingPoint maintains the player's nearby dockable references and owns the remote transform that attaches or detaches the player.
required.minimum: Evidence must connect docking-range membership and the attach/detach primitive to the player state.

required.reason: Cargo converts dock events into Station unloading or Asteroid mining and requests a forced undock when its weak reference is invalid or mining returns zero.
required.minimum: Evidence must distinguish the Station and Asteroid branches and their invalid-target behavior.

required.reason: Asteroid owns resource subtraction, mined emission, depletion detection, and the visual/freeing sequence that makes the dock target disappear.
required.minimum: Evidence must explain how an exhausted asteroid causes the cargo loop to stop and the dock to be released.

supporting.reason: Station is the unloading destination and owns the threshold check that turns delivered iron into an upgrade-unlocked event.

cases.notes: Bounded docking-to-resource-flow mechanism with explicit live-reference and depletion behavior.

cases.query: Which components own minimap viewport setup, admission of spawned world objects, proxy creation and tracking, map presentation, and the input and camera behavior that toggles the map?

required.reason: GameInitializer wires spawn signals, forwards world objects to pirates, initializes the HUD, and gives the camera the MapView and distortion setup hooks.
required.minimum: Evidence must establish the scene-level coordinator rather than assigning spawn ownership to the minimap itself.

required.reason: MapView owns the minimap viewport's camera and sprite container and filters spawned nodes into map proxies.
required.minimum: Evidence must identify the viewport-side registration and mini-map group admission rules.

required.reason: MapSprite owns the proxy's icon styling and RemoteTransform2D link to the source object.
required.minimum: Evidence must identify proxy tracking as a separate responsibility from viewport registration.

required.reason: The gameplay camera creates and registers the duplicate minimap camera and owns zoom animation in response to map events.
required.minimum: Evidence must connect map events to the gameplay camera's zoom behavior.

required.reason: MapDisplay owns the screen-facing ViewportTexture path, appear/disappear animations, audio, and map_toggled emission.
required.minimum: Evidence must distinguish presentation and event emission from camera zoom ownership.

required.reason: The UI layer owns the toggle-map input gate and prevents toggling while the display animation is running.
required.minimum: Evidence must locate the input boundary that invokes the display behavior.

cases.notes: Ownership control for a distributed minimap feature with scene coordinator, viewport, proxy, presentation, camera, and input seams.

cases.query: Trace a pirate squad from spawn setup through leader arrival and rest, patrol timing, aggro into attack, firing and pursuit cutoff, and the transitions that return the squad to patrol or rest.

required.reason: The spawner instantiates the group, assigns leader, cluster, squad, and faction data, adds each ship, and emits pirate_spawned.
required.minimum: Evidence must start at group construction and initialization rather than at an already-running pirate state.

required.reason: PirateShip owns squad relationships, emits aggro on area entry or damage, and converts the leader-target event into an Attack transition.
required.minimum: Evidence must connect squad-wide aggro routing to the state-machine transition.

required.reason: Spawn drives the leader or follows it to the cluster, emits reached_cluster, and transitions to Rest.
required.minimum: Evidence must include the initial arrival handoff for both leader and squaddie behavior.

required.reason: Rest owns the randomized wait and begins Patrol for the leader and matching squaddies.
required.minimum: Evidence must identify the timed Rest-to-Patrol handoff and its signal fan-out.

required.reason: Patrol builds or refreshes the path, applies steering, and returns the squad to Rest when the patrol timer ends.
required.minimum: Evidence must cover patrol path setup and both leader and squaddie exit behavior.

required.reason: Attack installs pursue, avoid, face, and separation behaviors, fires while aligned, and returns the leader or squad to Patrol after the pursuit limit.
required.minimum: Evidence must connect aggro entry to combat behavior and the cutoff back to patrol.

supporting.reason: The generic StateMachine performs the exit, state assignment, and enter sequence used by every pirate transition.
The Events singleton carries the leader-to-squad signals used by the state handoffs.

cases.notes: Ordered multi-state call chain spanning construction, squad signals, steering states, combat, and return transitions.

cases.query: Explain how mined iron reaches the station, crosses the upgrade threshold, pauses the game for a five-choice menu, routes the selected choice through the global signal bus, applies health, speed, cargo, mining, or weapon modifiers, and starts another pirate group at the largest unoccupied asteroid cluster.

required.reason: Cargo's unloading branch transfers cargo to Station.accumulated_iron while its docking handler selects unloading for stations and mining for asteroids.
required.minimum: Evidence must establish the resource-delivery start of the upgrade loop.

required.reason: Station resets the accumulated amount, emits upgrade_unlocked at the threshold, and raises the next threshold multiplier.
required.minimum: Evidence must include threshold crossing and repeat-upgrade behavior.

required.reason: UpgradeMenu binds each button to an enum choice, pauses and presents the menu, then unpauses and emits the selected upgrade.
required.minimum: Evidence must cover choice binding, pause state, signal emission, and menu teardown.

required.reason: Events defines the choice enum and the signals that connect Station, the menu, the player, and the world.
required.minimum: Evidence must identify the shared contract that carries unlock and selection events.

required.reason: PlayerShip maps the five choices to health, movement, cargo, mining, unload, damage, and cooldown stat modifiers.
required.minimum: Evidence must account for each upgrade branch and its target resource or stat.

required.reason: Stats owns modifier storage, recomputation from base values, and change notification used by upgraded resources and UI.
required.minimum: Evidence must explain how the selected modifier becomes a calculated stat rather than merely naming the player handler.

required.reason: GameWorld selects the largest free cluster and starts a new pirate group whenever an upgrade is chosen.
required.minimum: Evidence must connect selection to cluster choice and the new pirate-spawn request.

supporting.reason: PirateSpawner is the world-owned implementation that materializes the group requested by GameWorld.
StatsCargo exposes the cargo-specific calculated values and clamps the stored cargo quantity.

cases.notes: Long mixed flow combining resource accounting, UI state, event routing, typed upgrade mutation, stat recomputation, and world-side enemy spawning.
